teachingPoint: IR-T215-1 — T215 WebhookIngestionHandler MUST use crypto.timingSafeEqual() for HMAC signature verification (CF-211). String === comparison on HMAC digests leaks response timing that enables replay enumeration attacks. On invalid HMAC the handler MUST return HTTP 200 with no event emitted — returning 401 or 403 gives attackers an oracle confirming the signature was inspected.
positiveExample: T215 retrieves the signing secret via F427, computes the HMAC, then calls crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(expected, 'hex'), Buffer.from(actual, 'hex')). On false it returns HTTP 200 immediately with no WebhookEventIngested emitted.
negativeExample: T215 does if (expectedSig === actualSig) — string equality leaks timing; or returns 401 on mismatch — gives attackers confirmation that the HMAC path was reached.
discriminatingConstraint: timingSafeEqual required for HMAC comparison; HTTP 200 with no event on invalid — string === or 401/403 response = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: HMAC timingSafeEqual webhook ingestion replay attack CF-211 T215 constant time comparison http 200 no event invalid signature

teachingPoint: IR-T214-1 — T214 EtlSyncSagaHandler and T216 BackfillCoordinator MUST follow the EP-4 durable saga cycle order: rate_check → poll_page → land_raw → commit_cursor. Committing the cursor before landing raw causes data loss on crash recovery because the engine believes pages were processed. Skipping the rate check before polling wastes already-consumed API quota. The cursor MUST be validated monotonically increasing via ICursorCheckpointService (CF-193) at commit step — non-monotonic cursor indicates replay and must be rejected.
positiveExample: T214: F430.checkRateLimit() at ORDER 1 → pollPage() at ORDER 2 → storeDocument(rawRecord, {append-only}) at ORDER 3 → cursorCheckpoint.commitIfMonotonic(cursor) at ORDER 4. SyncJobFailed includes lastCursorPosition for crash recovery.
negativeExample: T214 commits cursor at ORDER 2 before landing raw at ORDER 3 — crash between ORDER 2 and 3 causes data loss. Or T214 polls first and rate-checks after — quota is consumed before the guard fires.
discriminatingConstraint: rate_check ORDER 1, poll ORDER 2, land_raw ORDER 3, commit_cursor ORDER 4 — any reorder or missing step = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: EP-4 durable saga rate_check poll_page land_raw commit_cursor cursor monotonic CF-193 ICursorCheckpointService T214 T216 sync backfill crash recovery

teachingPoint: IR-T219-1 — T219 DimensionalModelBuilder MUST use version-only SCD-2 strategy (DR-62). Direct UPDATE on dimension records is prohibited — it destroys the historical audit trail. The close_old_version + open_new_version operations MUST be atomic in a single transaction. If close succeeds but open fails, orphaned dim records (old version with no successor) corrupt the dimension history. effectiveFrom and effectiveTo MUST be set on all version records.
positiveExample: T219: (1) load existing open version → (2) atomic batch: storeDocument(closeOld, {effectiveTo: now}) + storeDocument(openNew, {effectiveFrom: now}) → (3) emit DimensionVersionCreated after atomic commit. Both storeDocument calls in one transaction.
negativeExample: T219 calls db.updateDocument({id: dimId, ...newFields}) — destroys previous state; or closes old in one transaction and opens new in a separate transaction — crash between creates orphaned dim record with no successor.
discriminatingConstraint: close_old + open_new atomic in one transaction — any direct UPDATE or non-atomic pair = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: SCD-2 version-only dimensional model builder close old open new atomic effectiveFrom effectiveTo T219 DR-62 no UPDATE orphaned

teachingPoint: IR-T220-1 — T220 MartKpiBuilder MUST classify ALL fields via IPiiClassificationService (F462) BEFORE writing to mart zone (DR-63). Mart data is aggregated cross-entity data — unmasked PII in the mart is the highest-risk data surface. If piiFieldsDetected > 0 without masking, the mart write MUST be blocked entirely. PiiClassificationCompleted MUST be emitted before MartRefreshed. RLS (F463) applied to all mart query results.
positiveExample: T220: F462.classify(allKpiFields) at ORDER 1 → if piiFieldsDetected > 0 && !masked return DataProcessResult.failure → computeKpis → storeDocument(mart) → emit PiiClassificationCompleted → emit MartRefreshed with piiGateApplied: true.
negativeExample: T220 calls F462.classify() after mart write — PII fields already persisted before classification. Or T220 skips F462 for numeric-only KPI fields assuming they cannot contain PII — aggregated counts can indirectly re-identify individuals.
discriminatingConstraint: F462.classify() at ORDER 1 before any mart write — any skip or post-write classification = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: PII gate mart write F462 IPiiClassificationService piiFieldsDetected block T220 MartKpiBuilder DR-63 F463 RLS PiiClassificationCompleted MartRefreshed

teachingPoint: IR-T221-1 — T221 IdentityJoinResolver MUST validate that ALL join inputs carry tenantId before any identity resolution (CF-204). A cross-tenant join — inputs from different tenants in the same resolution request — MUST be rejected immediately with CrossTenantJoinAttempted error. The guard runs at ORDER 1, before any DB access. RLS (F463) applied to results after resolution. IdentityJoinCompleted MUST include crossTenantGuardPassed: true.
positiveExample: T221: validateCrossTenantJoinInputs() at ORDER 1 verifies all inputs share the same tenantId from AsyncLocalStorage → cross-tenant mismatch throws CrossTenantJoinAttempted → resolve identities → F463.filter() → emit IdentityJoinCompleted with crossTenantGuardPassed: true.
negativeExample: T221 validates tenantId on primary input only, not on all join partners — a compromised tenant injects inputs from a second tenant into the join; the guard passes for the primary but the secondary leaks.
discriminatingConstraint: cross-tenant guard on ALL inputs at ORDER 1 — any join without tenantId on every input = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: cross tenant join guard T221 IdentityJoinResolver CF-204 tenantId ALL inputs validation F463 RLS crossTenantGuardPassed

teachingPoint: IR-T223-1 — T223 ReverseEtlPushHandler MUST push to external SaaS destinations via QUEUE_FABRIC exclusively — no direct HTTP (DR-64). Rate limit check via F430 MUST run BEFORE the push lock check. On throttle, RateLimitExhausted MUST be emitted — never silently dropped. ReverseETLPushed MUST include transport: 'queue_fabric' to prove the fabric path was used.
positiveExample: T223: F430.checkRateLimit() → checkPushLock() → queue.enqueue(pushJob) → storeDocument(pushRecord) → emit ReverseETLPushed with transport: 'queue_fabric'. On throttle emit RateLimitExhausted before returning.
negativeExample: T223 calls fetch() or axios.post() directly to the SaaS API endpoint — bypasses queue fabric transport contract. Or T223 silently returns DataProcessResult.success() on rate limit without emitting RateLimitExhausted.
discriminatingConstraint: queue.enqueue() only for SaaS push, RateLimitExhausted on throttle — any direct HTTP or silent drop = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: reverse ETL queue fabric only no direct HTTP T223 ReverseEtlPushHandler DR-64 F430 rate limit RateLimitExhausted transport queue_fabric
